title: calamophyllum teretiusculum
intro: Calamophyllum teretiusculum, also known as the "New Zealand tea tree," is a small shrub native to New Zealand, characterized by its woody stems and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Calamophyllum teretiusculum include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, ointments, or infusions, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
